attacking_style: Attacks decisively with 3-10km remaining on steep final climbs, favoring sustained accelerations over repeated punches. Comfortable going solo from distance when in top form (8-10km on Tourmalet, 4.8km at Bejes).
team_tactics: Relies on Jumbo-Visma high-tempo climbing trains to isolate rivals before launching. Uniquely willing to work as super-domestique for teammates despite being strongest rider. Team sometimes sacrifices his positioning for stage wins early in races.
terrain_preference: Excels on sustained steep gradients (9-10%+) and long alpine passes. Devastating time trialist on mixed terrain, even climbing at 9.4% on TT bike. Struggles on explosive punchy finishes under 500m and inside-line sprint accelerations.
vulnerability: Weak in final-kilometer sprint kicks and tactical positioning errors (gave Pogačar inside line, cracked in 500m accelerations). Early-season form incomplete. Susceptible to very steep Cat-1 pitches steeper than sustained alpine gradients. Cannot match explosive uphill sprint accelerations under 150m.
key_patterns: Attacks at 3-10km to go on steep final climbs, not summit sprints (multiple Tour/Vuelta stages)
Dominates sustained TT efforts, gaining 52sec on Pogačar mid-race in 2023 ITT (Tour 2023 ITT)
Willingly sacrifices GC for team tactics, working for Kuss/Sepp despite superior form (Vuelta 2023 multiple)
Struggles final 500m explosive kicks, loses bonus seconds to faster finishers (Tour 2023 stages)
Form trajectory shows mid-race improvement; weak early stages, dominant week 2-3 (Vuelta/Tour patterns)
Teams deploy him in sprint leadouts early-race, protecting GC positioning secondary initially (Dauphine 2023)
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta showed Tour de France form carried over despite 6-week gap. Recent observations show Tour fatigue erased and attacking strength returning on steep finishes by late Vuelta stages.
